mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which triggers risk-off portfolio rebalancing and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-interest-rate environment, rising consumer credit balances indicate unsustainable debt-fueled demand that eventually forces corporate margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising RSAFS under high FEDFUNDS conditions correlates with positive or neutral SPX forward returns in >50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust corporate hiring and revenue expansion, which mechanically supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS changes fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns in >60% of instances over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading exceeding 3.5% annualized triggers inflation hedging flows into cyclical equities, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate away from fixed income.
failure_condition: CPI readings above the threshold consistently precede negative SPX forward returns, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward analysis.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces real consumer credit expansion, it signals supply-side dominance that eases input cost pressures and improves corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence between INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns, or the signal's information coefficient falls below 0.02 over any rolling 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP contraction, a stable unemployment rate indicates structural labor demand rather than cyclical weakness, which supports defensive equity positioning and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Stable UNRATE during negative GDPC1 growth periods correlates with negative SPX forward returns, or the hypothesis fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in out-of-sample testing.
